name: Cold Glazed Salmon
recipeIngredient: 7 lb Whole salmon, cleaned and rinsed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine the white wine, basil, 3 tarragon sprigs, shallots, rosemary, lemon slices, and celery leaves. Simmer the mixture for 20 minutes or until reduced to about 3 tablespoons.
2. Lay the salmon on a piece of heavy foil twice as long as the fish. Fold the edges over the fish and secure tightly.
3. Place the package in a preheated 375°F oven and bake for 50-60 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
4. Open the foil carefully and remove the skin from the top of the salmon below the head to the tail. Scrape away any brown flesh, leaving the head and tail intact.
5. Drain the liquid from the foil and invert the fish onto a platter. Remove the foil and skin, then prepare the other side in the same manner.
6. Chill the fish, covered, overnight.
7. Peel the turnip and cut into thin slices. Arrange them on a plate and cover with ice water until ready to use.
8. Mash the egg yolk with butter and let cool at room temperature.
9. Brush a thin coat of liquid fish aspic over the salmon and arrange the tarragon sprigs and turnip slices decoratively on top.
10. Brush additional liquid fish aspic over the whole fish.
11. Pipe the egg yolk mixture into the centers of the turnip flowers using a pastry bag fitted with a decorative tip.
12. Chill the salmon for at least 2 hours, up to 6 hours before serving.
Served surrounded by chilled aspic and chopped garnish (1964 Gourmet Magazine favorite).
